channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Fox News Criticizes Trump's Iran Policy Amid Political Concerns
item.description: The article discusses how prominent Fox News commentators are growing critical of President Donald Trump's handling of the Iran conflict, highlighting political and military challenges. Britt Hume, a ...

item.title: UK government borrowing lower than expected in June
item.description: The UK government borrowed £16bn in June, slightly below the £16.3bn forecast by the Office for Budget Responsibility (OBR), according to official data. This marks a £7.9bn reduction compared to June ...

item.title: Brisbane Lions' Future Uncertain as Key Players and Coach Face Contract Decisions
item.description: The Brisbane Lions face critical decisions as key players Kai Lohmann and Logan Morris approach contract expirations, with coach Chris Fagan's future also in question. Lohmann's stellar form, includin...

item.title: Common myna's journey from pest control to invasive species in Australia
item.description: The common myna, originally brought to Melbourne in 1862 as a biological control agent for crop pests, has since become one of the world’s most notorious invasive bird species. Initially introduced to...

item.title: CBS News Last-Minute Decision to Air Trump Speech Surprises Staff
item.description: CBS News faced internal confusion and surprise when it abruptly changed its plan to cover Donald Trump's national address. Initially, staffers were told the speech would only be available on the netwo...

item.title: Saudi Arabia Introduces One-Year Multiple-Entry Umrah Visa for Pilgrims
item.description: The Saudi Ministry of Haj and Umrah has introduced a new multiple-entry Umrah visa valid for one year, allowing pilgrims to visit the kingdom multiple times with a cumulative stay of up to 90 days ann...

item.title: Nine cuts newspaper jobs, blames AI amid staff redundancies and political controversies
item.description: The article details Nine's decision to implement redundancies across its publishing division, attributing the move to an 'extreme state of disruption' caused by AI. The company's publishing boss, Tory...

item.title: UK Boosts Africa Infrastructure with £10bn Investment
item.description: The article discusses UK Export Finance's £10bn commitment to African infrastructure projects, emphasizing collaboration with British suppliers, bankable borrowers, and adherence to climate rules. Tim...

item.title: Sri Lanka Combats Dengue Outbreak with Military Support and Drone Surveillance
item.description: Sri Lanka is facing its worst dengue outbreak in nearly a decade, with over 76,044 infections and 53 deaths reported since January 2026. The government has deployed military forces and air force drone...

item.title: Fatal Collision on Abeokuta-Ibadan Expressway Claims Two Lives
item.description: A tragic road accident claimed two lives on the Abeokuta-Ibadan Expressway in Ogun State. The incident, attributed to a speed violation, occurred around 5:45 PM when a black Lexus car collided with a ...

item.title: Tanzania's Customer Satisfaction Scores Rise Significantly
item.description: Tanzania has achieved a significant milestone in service delivery, with customer satisfaction scores climbing 17.6 percentage points over five years, according to the Tanzania Customer Service Index (...

item.title: Baptist Nazareth Church Leader Invites AmaZulu King Misuzulu for Tea After Reconciliation Efforts
item.description: The Baptist Nazareth Church leader, Mduduzi Shembe, has invited AmaZulu King Misuzulu kaZwelithini to visit for tea as part of reconciliation efforts following a controversial incident where the King ...

item.title: PM Modi's Response to NEET Paper Leak and Protests Over Education Mismanagement
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi asserted that the Union government has taken stringent measures to prevent future exam paper leaks, following allegations of the NEET-UG paper leak. Parliamentary Affairs ...

item.title: NTSA arrests 22 pedestrians for ignoring footbridge rules
item.description: NTSA, National Police Service, and Nairobi City County Government jointly arrested 22 pedestrians for crossing roads instead of using footbridges. The suspects were charged with obstructing traffic fl...

item.title: Philippines Condemns China's Actions in West Philippine Sea Amid Diplomatic Tensions
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has summoned Chinese Ambassador Jing Quan to Malacañang following a reported clash between Chinese Coast Guard personnel and a Philippine Navy officer at Ayungin Shoal. ...

item.title: Two Alberta Girls Experience Unique RCMP Command Role Through Kids with Cancer Program
item.description: Two Alberta girls, Maya Ferguson (10) and Haley Nedeljak (11), participated in a special experience where they temporarily assumed command roles within the Alberta RCMP. The initiative, organized by t...

item.title: ADC Calls for Transparency in N7.13 Trillion Energy Security Spending
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has called for full transparency regarding the N7.13 trillion recorded as 'energy security' expenditure in the 2024 audited accounts of the Nigerian National Petr...

item.title: Prince Harry's New Travel Boss Warns Against Comparing Flying to Drink-Driving
item.description: The article highlights Prince Harry's final appearance at the FIFA World Cup with his fiancée Georgina Rodriguez, following which he was appointed as a new travel boss. The piece critiques the compari...

item.title: DepEd and Australia Launch P1.6-B Program to Enhance SHS Students' Job Readiness
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) and the Australian government have launched a P1.6-billion initiative aimed at improving senior high school (SHS) education and better preparing graduates for emplo...

item.title: U.S. and China Compete in Open-Source AI Development
item.description: The article discusses the growing competition between the United States and China in the field of open-source artificial intelligence (AI). Both nations are investing heavily in AI research and develo...
